name: arbor-vscode
description: Impact analysis, refactor prediction, and AI-aware code navigation powered by semantic dependency graphs

arbor.serverUrl.description: WebSocket URL for the Arbor Sync Server

arbor.autoIndex.description: Automatically re-index workspace on file save

arbor.maxBlastRadius.description: Maximum blast radius threshold before warning

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Arbor
walkthroughs.description: Set up graph-native code intelligence in your workspace

steps.title: Index Your Codebase
steps.description:
Run `arbor setup` in your terminal or use the command palette.

[Run Arbor Index](command:arbor.index)

media.markdown:
## First, build the graph

Arbor parses your code into a semantic dependency graph. Every function, class, and import becomes a node with real edges.

```bash
arbor setup
```

steps.title: Analyze Impact
steps.description:
Right-click any function and select **Arbor: Analyze Impact** to see what breaks before you change it.

[Try Impact Analysis](command:arbor.refactor)

media.markdown:
## See the blast radius

Before refactoring, Arbor tells you:
- How many callers depend on this symbol
- Which files need updates
- The full transitive impact

steps.title: Git-Aware Workflows
steps.description:
Use `arbor diff` to preview the impact of your uncommitted changes.

[Run Git Diff Impact](command:arbor.diff)

media.markdown:
## Predict before you push

`arbor diff` analyzes your git changes and shows:
- Changed symbols and their callers
- API entrypoints affected
- Files likely requiring updates

steps.title: Connect AI Agents (MCP)
steps.description:
Arbor serves as an MCP tool so Claude, Cursor, and other AI agents get structural code understanding.

```bash
claude mcp add --transport stdio --scope project arbor -- arbor bridge
```

media.markdown:
## Give AI agents real code intelligence

Instead of keyword search, AI agents traverse your actual dependency graph for precise, deterministic answers.
